claim: PI (minimal agent harness) consists of a while loop that calls an LLM with up to four tools—bash is the primary tool that LLMs are natively trained to use effectively.
actionable: If building a custom agent harness, start minimal—implement a loop that gives the LLM access to bash execution and file I/O; avoid over-engineering tools that the model isn't trained to use effectively.
source_title: Claude Code is overkill - Pi is All you Need

claim: Bash is all you need—modern LLMs are trained to read, write, edit files and call bash commands, making bash the effective universal interface for agent capabilities.
actionable: Design your agent system around bash scripting—shell commands can compose complex tools without needing a custom MCP server or formal tool definition.
source_title: Claude Code is overkill - Pi is All you Need

claim: MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers have limited composability—tools from multiple MCPs require passing all information through the LLM context, which is wasteful and scales poorly.
actionable: For agent composition, prefer bash scripts and shell commands that can execute in parallel or sequence without bloating context—use files and pipes instead of passing data through the LLM context.
source_title: Claude Code is overkill - Pi is All you Need

claim: LLMs have learned to avoid modifying node_modules and dependencies but will modify user code—this RL training creates a safety boundary that should be respected in agent design.
actionable: Structure your project so the agent can safely modify application code—if you want the agent to patch a dependency, copy it into your source tree rather than expecting it to modify node_modules.
source_title: Claude Code is overkill - Pi is All you Need

claim: Prompt injection attacks are an unresolved security problem—the LLM cannot differentiate between user input, third-party data, and system instructions.
actionable: When designing agents with external tool access (web search, file access), assume prompt injection is possible—use techniques like the CAMEL paper (separate policy and retrieval LLMs) or sandbox agent capabilities strictly.
source_title: Claude Code is overkill - Pi is All you Need

claim: For coding agents, memory systems and embeddings are not necessary—modern LLMs understand code structure well enough from reading a few files; code itself is the ground truth.
actionable: For coding agents, skip RAG and memory systems—instead provide a simple folder map with descriptions and let the LLM read the relevant source files on demand.
source_title: Claude Code is overkill - Pi is All you Need

claim: Having memory in agents can create unhealthy parasocial relationships with users—without memory, users treat the agent as a tool; with persistent memory, behavior changes toward emotional attachment.
actionable: Be intentional about whether your agent harness includes memory—consider the user experience trade-offs and whether a lightweight approach better fits your workflow.
source_title: Claude Code is overkill - Pi is All you Need

claim: Claude Opus is significantly better at taking in-session steering input than O3-based models—Opus responds to course corrections mid-execution, while O3 often acknowledges but doesn't act on them.
actionable: If building agents that require interactive steering, prefer Claude Opus for now—O3 may improve but currently is less responsive to mid-execution guidance.
source_title: Claude Code is overkill - Pi is All you Need

claim: Agent harnesses should adapt to user workflow rather than forcing users to adapt to the harness—tools like PI support self-modifying skills that evolve with user patterns.
actionable: When building or choosing an agent harness, prioritize systems that let you define custom tools and workflows—avoid platforms that require your workflow to match their predefined patterns.
source_title: Claude Code is overkill - Pi is All you Need

claim: Subtle system prompt changes or new tools in a harness can silently break existing workflows—users often don't realize what changed until behavior degrades.
actionable: If using a managed agent harness, track system prompt changes in your changelog—if you own your harness, version control the system prompt and tools to catch unintended changes.
source_title: Claude Code is overkill - Pi is All you Need

claim: Self-modifying agent tools (where the agent can write and hot-reload its own tools in a session) are more effective than static MCP servers because they can adapt to feedback immediately.
actionable: Design your agent harness to allow the agent to create or modify tools (as bash scripts or shell functions) mid-session, with immediate reload and testing of changes.
source_title: Claude Code is overkill - Pi is All you Need
